item.title: Traffic speed rises in city as PM, top officials follow signals: Report
item.description: Compliance with traffic signals by the Prime Minister and other top state officials has contributed to an increase in vehicle speed on the capital's roads, according to a report submitted to Prime Minister Tarique Rahman today. "A report on the reduction of traffic congestion in the capital and th

item.title: NBR invites budget proposals for FY27
item.description: The National Board of Revenue (NBR) has invited budget proposals from taxpayers and key stakeholders as it begins preparations for 2026-27 fiscal year(FY27), aiming to frame a participatory and pro-people revenue policy. In a press release issued on Tuesday, the revenue authority said it regularly

item.title: Govt directs factories to clear February wages within first seven working days of March
item.description: The government on Tuesday instructed all factory owners to pay workers’ wages for February within the seven working days of March and to disburse festival allowances by March 12. Factory authorities have also been asked to pay half of the March wages in advance, following discussions between

item.title: ACC chairman, two commissioners resign
item.description: Anti-Corruption Commission (ACC) Chairman Dr MA and two commissioners resigned from their posts on Tuesday. The two commissioners are Brig Hafiz Ahsan Faridi and Mia Mohammad Ali Akbar Azizi. Sources at the ACC said the chairman and commissioners cited personal reasons in their resignation letter

item.title: Muktadir stresses bringing all sectors under a formal structure
item.description: Commerce Minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir today (Tuesday) said that a large segment of Bangladesh's workforce still remains in the informal sector and stressed the need to gradually bring all sectors under a formal structure to ensure sustainable and balanced development. The Minister made the re

item.title: US suspends consular services across Pakistan amid security concerns
item.description: The United States has cancelled visa appointments at its embassy in Islamabad and consulates in Lahore and Karachi amid escalating unrest in Pakistan. In a post on X on Tuesday, the US Embassy said that due to the current security situation, all visa appointments at the embassy and the consulates

item.title: Japan commits $3.2m to sustain life-saving sexual, reproductive health services for Rohingyas
item.description: The Government of Japan has reaffirmed its strong commitment to the Rohingya humanitarian response by approving JPY 500 million (approximately USD 3.2 million) in funding to the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA). The new two-year project will aim at protecting the health and rights of w

item.title: 182 flights cancelled at Dhaka, Chattogram airports in 4 days of Middle East conflict
item.description: A total of 147 international flights at Dhaka's Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport and 35 flights at Chattogram's Shah Amanat International Airport have been cancelled over the last four days due to the onset of war in the Middle East. The two airport authorities confirmed the information in s

item.title: Progress on rules for lethal autonomous weapons urgently needed, says chair of Geneva talks
item.description: Progress on a potential international framework to prohibit and restrict Lethal Autonomous Weapons Systems (LAWS) is urgently needed, as talks on the matter in Geneva enter a crucial phase, their chair said. From this week to the mandate’s end in September, 128 states will discuss whether to

item.title: DSE records steepest decline in six years
item.description: The equity market index plunged 209 points on Tuesday as jittery investors dumped their holdings amid escalating Middle East conflict. The broad index of the Dhaka Stock Exchange, DSEX, fell 208.98 points to close at 5,325, down from 5,534 in the prior session. The DSE index plunged 3.78 per cent

item.title: Key Gaza crossing will be reopened, says World Food Programme
item.description: A key aid crossing ‌that had been momentarily closed into Gaza will reopen imminently, the United Nations World Food Programme said ​on Tuesday. Humanitarian supplies will be able ​to enter through the Kerem Shalom ⁠crossing into the enclave between Tuesday and ​Wednesday, the WFP said. "Tha

item.title: India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar outage, sources say
item.description:
Indian companies on Tuesday reduced natural gas supplies to industries in anticipation of tighter supply from ​the Middle East after top producer Qatar halted production, four ‌industry sources with knowledge of the matter said.
Qatar halted its liquefied natural gas production on Mon
